mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, so the S&P 500 should post positive returns over the next month when policy is easing. The signal fails if rate cuts occur during a recessionary collapse that overwhelms valuation support.
failure_condition: Triggered 20-day windows do not produce positive excess returns relative to baseline, or the sign of the return relation flips during easing episodes for more than one rolling year.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still elevated, markets infer restrictive real rates and tighter financial conditions, which compress future equity multiples and push the S&P 500 lower over the next month. The claim fails if high rates coincide with falling inflation but equities do not weaken.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL rising versus 3 months ago
failure_condition: In triggered observations, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive more than 60% of the time, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive value over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates deteriorating labor income prospects and weaker consumer spending, so the S&P 500 should decline over the next 20 trading days once unemployment rises by a meaningful margin. The signal fails if unemployment rises but equity drawdowns do not follow.
failure_condition: After the trigger, the median 20-day SPX return is not negative, or fewer than 55% of triggered cases are negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rise in PAYEMS and a fall in UNRATE signals broad labor-market resilience, supporting household income and consumption and lifting the S&P 500 over the next month. This mechanism fails if payroll gains are offset by rising slack or if labor strength is already fully priced.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change positive and UNRATE flat or lower
failure_condition: The combined labor-strength signal has no positive incremental effect on 20-day returns versus each proxy alone, or triggered periods fail to outperform baseline by at least 1 percentage point on average.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS reflects stronger retail demand and better near-term revenue expectations for cyclically exposed firms, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The claim fails if sales strength is accompanied by margin compression that negates revenue optimism.
failure_condition: Rolling walk-forward tests show no positive relation between RSAFS growth and 20-day SPX returns, or the sign is negative in more than half of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in INDPRO signals weakening industrial activity and softer corporate earnings momentum, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 20 trading days when production contracts. The signal fails if industrial weakness is offset by a strong policy response that rallies equities immediately.
failure_condition: Triggered windows do not exhibit negative median 20-day returns, or the trigger loses explanatory power after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent trend signals housing-sector contraction, weaker wealth effects, and lower future consumption, which should weigh on the S&P 500 over a quarter. The mechanism fails if housing weakness is isolated and equity investors ignore it because broader growth remains intact.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by more than 5%
failure_condition: Over 60-day horizons, triggered episodes do not show negative excess returns versus non-triggered periods, or the effect disappears in repeated out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and RSAFS is also improving, the economy is expanding on both aggregate output and consumer demand, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The claim fails if GDP growth is strong but sales weaken, indicating inventory distortion rather than real demand.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growing year over year and RSAFS rising from 3 months ago
failure_condition: The joint growth condition fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of cases, or the combined signal is not stronger than either proxy alone.
